codeReview.summary: The feature commit delivers the main guardrails it set out to add: duplicate connect attempts are suppressed, in-flight connects can be canceled cleanly, and the session UI exposes reconnect/cancel flows without introducing obvious zombie-session regressions. I did find one small race around immediate reconnect after cancel, but no blocking correctness issue.

issues.description: After `disconnect()` marks the UI as disconnected, `currentAttempt` is still kept until the original connect coroutine reaches its `finally` block, so an immediate reconnect tap in that window is dropped silently by the `currentAttempt != null` guard. The UI re-enables the host button as soon as `sessionState.isConnecting` becomes false (`feature/session/src/main/kotlin/io/github/jtsang4/aterm/feature/session/SessionEntryPoint.kt:247`), so users can hit a brief dead-tap period right after canceling a slow connect.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `ssh-terminal-worker` as fully followed, but the recorded lifecycle validation stopped at composable hide/show and fixture-level cancellation. For a feature explicitly targeting rotation/background interruption truthfulness, the transcript does not show the skill's required manual lifecycle exercise for rotation/background flows.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /root/workspace/github/jtsang4/aterm/.factory/skills/ssh-terminal-worker/SKILL.md step 7 requires manual lifecycle exercise for connect/disconnect/rotation/backgrounding; /root/.factory/missions/fc6a9d6d-b329-4b38-9f7c-ed2a18da2774/handoffs/2026-04-11T04-25-29-366Z__connection-lifecycle-and-session-guards__13986629-fb31-4335-8461-21a82155fb40.json records only unmount/remount and trust-prompt cancel checks.

summary: Pass with one non-blocking issue: the connection-lifecycle guards are sound overall, but canceling a slow connect can leave a short window where the UI looks reconnect-ready before the coordinator will accept a new connect attempt.
